text.title: Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) in CI/CD Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Full active scan launched at production URL; rate limits tripped; security monitoring alerts fire; customer-impacting throttles.
antipattern.root-cause: Author swapped the target URL during a 'quick test' and forgot to revert; or copy-pasted from production deploy config.
antipattern.fix: Add a CI-side guard: regex-block target_url containing prod-domain. Roll back any prod-pointing DAST workflow.

antipattern.symptom: ZAP spider follows links to a payment gateway and a SaaS email service; bogus support tickets generated; compliance breach.
antipattern.root-cause: Author launched with default config 'just to see what it finds'; never wrote the scope file.
antipattern.fix: Block PR until .zap/rules.tsv exists. Pre-load with the standard org-wide denylist (third-party hosts, payment paths, email endpoints).

antipattern.symptom: Nuclei nightly run flags 200 new issues; investigation shows a community template change inflated severity.
antipattern.root-cause: Workflow used `templates: latest` or omitted template-version.
antipattern.fix: Set `template-version: vX.Y.Z`. Bump on a defined schedule (monthly) after spot-check.

antipattern.symptom: DAST only runs on PR; staging drifts between deploys; CVE introduced by a base-image update goes uncaught for weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wired DAST as a manual / on-demand workflow.
antipattern.fix: Add at least one schedule entry (nightly or weekly). Run post-deploy AND scheduled.
